title: Meet MS
brief: Meet Mission Systems — a second interface running in parallel with FA. The Sentry radar boots through INITIALIZATION. Read its heartbeat, wait for it to report OPERATE, then confirm it with an on-demand status request.
teaches: MS is a separate body on its own bus, with its own heartbeat (SubsystemStatusMT / ServiceStatusMT, MS Vol §1.2.9.1/§1.2.9.4) — you read it exactly as you read FA, but the party is MS, not FA. Unlike FA, MS isn't safety-critical: it never validates or REJECTs your request. An on-demand SubsystemStatusDataRequestMT (MS Vol §1.2.9.5) sent too early just reflects the subsystem's current (non-OPERATE) state. Confirm OPERATE before relying on a capability.

objective._doc: No flight. Win = an on-demand status reply confirms SENTRY-RADAR-01 is OPERATE (ms-status). The subsystem boots INITIALIZATION and reaches OPERATE at tick 6; a request before then latches the non-OPERATE state and never wins.
